items.title: [正面] It's an indie so there is a couple huge disclaimer...
items.summary:
It's an indie so there is a couple huge disclaimers I'd like to get out of the way before the full review. It is currently mostly translated via chat GPT, They do have a translator now but almost the entire game is still translated this way. I've actually been reading the story and I'd say its understandable 99% of the time though chat GPTs inability to track who is a man or women can be annoying. It also sometimes translates thing differently in different spots.

The devs are very willing to buff/nerf units. They do give out compensation that lets you reset your characters to zero dupes/levels and refunds those with generic dupes you can use on other characters.

Gameplay you build your team of 4 units which once in game act as a single entity. Each units will bring their own set of 4 cards for your deck. Each also has their own "Exalt" ability which is just an ultimate that charges over time you can release when needed. The player character also has one of these that charges on the side, though the PC has no cards of their own for the deck. Most missions will have you searching a tile based map for a boss to kill. Some are just direct fights.

The play styles of characters varies pretty wildly. There are 4 factions that characters can belong to each with its own over arching themes and then the characters do varying amounts of interaction with this. You can have up to 2 factions in a deck. Chaos (Yellow) is dedicated to being splashed into other factions and has buffs based on which they join. Blue (Aqueor) calls on the power of abyss and does tentacle things. Red (Caro) primarily deal with body alteration. Vampires and such are here. They play high risk/reward stuff. Purple (ultra) uses the powers of space and time to beg for another buff. Surly just one more will make them playable.

The difficulty curve starts off rather easy but scales up in late game to be fairly brutal once you're in endgame. The numbers get very very large but its still quite manageable if you've built a good team with a solid theme/synergy.

The Gacha and monetization: Monetization oscillates between some of the best I've seen in a live service and years and being down right scams. Don't run away after this say as this game is way outside the norm on how they do the gacha, but I should say upfront the game is very dupe reliant and I think this is actually fine. I'm just going to go list form to explain this.
-The game guarantees 1 SR or better every 5 pulls instead of every 10
-Soft pity kicks in at 30 pulls. Soft pity is you immediately get an SSR. Hard pity occurs on your next SSR if you get 2 off banner SSRs
-We do have the typical 3 currency types. 1 for standard, 1 for limited, and 1 for either (which just means a second limited currency)
-The shop that uses in-game currency sells an item that can be used as a dupe of any character you own once a month. 
-Dupe breakpoints will always be at 1, 2, 3, and 7. Dupes cap out at 15 copies but outside the breakpoints dupes are only stat bonuses and not worth pursuing.
-There is a weapon Gacha. These are called Wheels of Destiny. Many of these do more interesting things then just being stat boosters. Some will add additional copies of cards while others add extra effects to cards, that kind of thing.
-The amount of pulls given to the player is extremely high. Each missions will give 1 standard pull + 1.2 pulls worth of the either type currency. Clearing a chapter gives you an additional 10 of the currency type. Maintenance they give out 2 pulls. The monthly pass gives 31.
-They do a lot of fomo packs. These are either dumpster tier scams or very good value. Though if you are going to only be a light spender its recommended to skip all of them.
-The battle pass (approx $10) has roughly 20 pulls as well as a ton of materials as well as exclusive SSR Wheels (weapons) once you have acquired one of the wheels you can get a dupe each month from the in game currency shop.

items.title: [正面] Its a pretty good game locked behinde paywalls. I ...
items.summary: Its a pretty good game locked behinde paywalls. I like how the game plays but its a mobilephone game on PC and I feel that playing it without paying for uppgrades and characters is abit hard and grindy. Hopfully my English isnt so bad and you people understan my lousy review.
